regime: Risk-On Trend / Whipsaw Resolves Up (Equities Confirm the Long Flip)
summary: The three-session whipsaw finally resolved, and it resolved up. MNQ, MES and MGC all closed higher on rising long stops, the first clean trend day in a week. MNQ ripped +1.84% to 29,993, kissing the 30,000 psych; MES pressed to 7,595, seven ticks under its 7,602 cash record; gold firmed its recovery +1.08%. Volatility normalized as the move ran (MNQ ATR14 158 to 119), the mark of an orderly trend rather than an expansion. Crude was the lone outlier, reversing 3.91% and flipping its 1hr stop short. The book can lift from DEFENSIVE toward REDUCED, but it is day one of the trend and CPI (June) next week is the gate.

keyLevels.label: MNQ ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Rising LONG; RTH close +292 above. The line that now defines the trend.

keyLevels.label: MNQ AVWAP / RTH High
keyLevels.note: Closed above AVWAP; the 29,993 high sits seven points under the 30,000 psych.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop / Record
keyLevels.note: Long; the 7,595 high stalled seven ticks under the 7,602 cash record.

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop / AVWAP
keyLevels.note: Long firmed; ran to a 4,177 high, +25 above the stop.

keyLevels.label: MCL ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED SHORT; close 71.83, -1.72 below after a -3.9% reversal.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (long)
setups.entry: Long with the trend on a pullback to the rising AVWAP (29,901), not a chase into the 30,000 psych
setups.bias: IN_HABITAT long. First clean trend day after three chop sessions; still respect that it is day one.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce (MNQ / MES)
setups.entry: Long on a hold of the rising session AVWAP (MNQ 29,901, MES 7,579) as support
setups.t1: RTH high, then the psych / record above
setups.bias: IN_HABITAT. AVWAP is rising support on a trend day; a loss is the first stall signal.

setups.name: E3v2 Momentum Long (15min)
setups.entry: Long on a 15min breakout with the regime filter green
setups.bias: IN_HABITAT (re-armed). Risk-on trend qualifies the filter; best breakout tape in a week.

event.name: No scheduled Tier-1 release (weekly jobless claims 8:30 ET, minor)
event.actual: Weekly jobless claims printed roughly in line and were a market non-event; price action, not a catalyst, drove the trend day.
event.nqReaction: MNQ held its rising 29,637 stop all session, broke the 29,871 OR high on the 13:50 UTC opening drive and ran to 29,993, kissing the 30,000 psych. A clean trend-continuation day, the follow-through the Jul 8 squeeze needed.
event.crossAsset: Equities and gold trended up together (MES +0.94% to 7,595 near the 7,602 record; MGC +1.08% to a 4,177 high). Crude was the outlier, reversing 3.91% and flipping its 1hr stop short at 73.55.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: Strong trend close near the highs; a Friday gap up would run straight into the 30,000 psych, no clean fade yet.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The long-horizon base and the short-horizon trend are now aligned long after the whipsaw resolved up.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES and MNQ both trended and closed green on rising stops; a genuine both-long this time, not a stop-ratchet artifact.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED (up from DEFENSIVE). The whipsaw resolved into a clean trend day; edges can size up modestly, but it is day one of the trend.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding; the book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.sizing: A TSMOM-20 long is now with the trend and above a rising 29,637 stop; a starter-to-half is reasonable on a pullback to the AVWAP rather than a chase into the 30,000 psych. The crude short is a fresh signal but late in the day.
risk.note: Week ahead: CPI (June) next week is the gate on this trend. Friday is a normal session; let pullbacks to the rising AVWAP set entries rather than chasing the round number.

psychology: The first clean trend day after three whipsaws tempts two mistakes: chasing the 30,000 round number at the highs, or distrusting the move because the last three flips burned you. Neither is process. The trend is defined by the rising 29,637 stop; trade with it, enter on pullbacks to the AVWAP, and let the round number come to you. One clean day is not a reason to abandon the confirmation discipline that kept you flat through the chop.

DirectionRISK-ON TREND / CONFIRMMedium-HighMNQ +1.84%, MES +0.94%, MGC +1.08% all closed up on rising long stops. First clean trend day after three chop sessions; the whipsaw resolved to the upside. Crude the lone outlier, flipping short.

Trend edgeTSMOM-20 long confirmed on both indices via price, not a stop ratchet; the Jul 8 flip held and extended.
ContinuationQualifies long on pullbacks to the rising AVWAP (MNQ 29,901), not a chase into the 30,000 psych.
E3v2 Momentum LongRe-armed and qualifying in the risk-on trend; the 15min breakout filter is green.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html:
LevelValue
Center (AVWAP)29,901
Sigma70
+1s / +2s / +3s29,966 / 30,036 / 30,107
-1s / -2s / -3s29,825 / 29,755 / 29,684
SignalPrice rode the +1s band up on a trend day; mean-reversion fades are counter-trend and OUT of habitat today. A fade needs a stretched +3s (30,107) tag with an exhaustion bar, half size at most. Price-based (volume unavailable on the continuous contract).

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked
fullReport.html:

1MNQ holds above its 29,055 flipped-long stop and reclaims the 29,386 OR high on a 2-closeTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTHeld 29,055 all day, blew through the OR high and ran to 29,993, tagging both the 29,558 O/N target and the 29,638 +3s.
2MNQ 2-closes back below the 29,055 stopNOT TRIGGEREDNever threatened; the RTH low was 29,614, nearly 560 above the stop.
3MES holds its 7,472 stop and reclaims the 7,523 OR highTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTHeld 7,472, reclaimed 7,523 and ran to 7,595, tagging the 7,563 O/N high and stalling seven ticks under the 7,602 record.
4MCL holds above its 73.58 trailing stop and the 74.00 psychTRIGGERED / FAILEDHeld the stop overnight to 75.15 then reversed hard through 73.58 and 74.00 to a 71.83 close; the stop flipped short. First failed row of the month.
5MGC reclaims its 4,069 stop and the 4,102 AVWAP with acceptanceTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTHeld 4,069, accepted over the 4,102 AVWAP and ran to 4,177, tagging the 4,130 target and the 4,175 O/N high.

Score: 5 rows, 4 triggered (3 played out, 1 failed), 1 not triggered. July MTD: 16 triggered / 9 played out / 1 failed.

Filters Friday: Friday is a normal session (not FOMC, not Monday, no CPI), so HOF, MID, BIR and BNC are all eligible. Calendar: no Tier-1 US print scheduled Friday Jul 10; CPI (June) lands next week.

Row 4 TRIGGERED / FAILED (crude lost its 73.58 stop and the 74.00 psych, reversed to 71.83 and flipped short). Row 2 NOT TRIGGERED (MNQ never neared the 29,055 stop).
